executive_summary: The Iran–US–Israel conflict has entered an unprecedented escalation phase following the confirmed killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei in US-Israeli airstrikes on 28 February. Iran has responded with mass missile and drone strikes on 14+ US military bases across Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Qatar, plus ballistic missile barrages against Israel. The conflict now directly involves all major Gulf states, with regional instability spreading to Pakistan (protests, 10 deaths near US Consulate), Iraq (embassy attacks), and Cyprus (Iranian missile launches). Threat level remains MAJOR with 171 major incidents in the last 24 hours.

Iran strikes two US bases in Riyadh and Eastern Saudi Arabia — direct escalation against US assets in the Gulf
Suicide attacks targeting US naval command headquarters in Bahrain reported — ongoing situation

Kuwait intercepts hostile targets in southern airspace — Gulf states actively defending against Iranian attacks

Air raid sirens sounding in Tel Aviv amid ongoing Iranian missile attacks

Gulf states (Saudi Arabia, Kuwait) condemn Iranian attacks, coordinate defensive responses

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is expected to continue escalating over the next 24-72 hours. Iran has demonstrated capacity and willingness to conduct mass missile strikes across the region, and with the Supreme Leader's death, the IRGC may pursue vengeance without traditional restraint. US and Israel will likely continue intensive strikes on Iranian military infrastructure. The 14 US bases struck will prompt retaliatory US responses. Regional spread is likely — Pakistan protests and Cyprus attacks indicate expanding geographic scope. Gulf states face continued Iranian missile and drone threats. Civilian casualty incidents (schools, volleyball players) may further escalate Iranian domestic pressure for response.
escalation_risks: Iranian retaliation against Gulf states intensifies — more missile/drone strikes on Saudi, Kuwait, UAE, Qatar infrastructure
Israeli ground or expanded air campaign into Iran — potential for regime change operations
US military escalation — additional carrier groups, increased strikes on Iranian targets
Regional wider war — Hezbollah involvement, Syrian/Iraqi proxy activation, Yemen Houthi escalation
Civilian casualty escalations — Iranian strikes on Israeli population centers, potential counter-strikes on Iranian cities
Cyber attacks on Gulf oil infrastructure — supply chain disruption risks
Russian/Chinese involvement — Putin condemned US/Israel, potential for indirect support to Iran
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation appears unlikely in the 24-72h window given the current trajectory. The killing of Khamenei represents a point-of-no-return that will likely trigger continued Iranian retaliation regardless of diplomatic overtures. US statements that ally safety is 'not conditional' suggest no immediate pullback. Regional actors (Egypt, Oman, Jordan) are engaged in diplomatic efforts but lack leverage over either side. A ceasefire would require either: complete Iranian surrender of military capacity, US/Israel acceptance of Iranian retaliation as 'sufficient,' or third-party强制调解 (likely requiring Russian or Chinese intervention). The humanitarian cost is mounting rapidly, potentially building international pressure for ceasefire discussions within 72-96 hours.
